channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Court Grants Defense Access To DSS Director-General In Cybercrime Trial Over Social Media Posts
item.description: The Federal High Court in Abuja has allowed Omoyele Sowore’s defense team to summon the Director-General of the Department of State Services (DSS), Tosin Ajayi, to testify in his ongoing cybercrime tr...

item.title: House Speaker Urges Protection of Food-Producing Provinces for National Food Security
item.description: House Speaker Faustino Dy III has called on the government to prioritize the protection of food-producing provinces to ensure national food security. During a briefing on El Niño preparedness, Dy high...

item.title: Tom Morello Honors Late Mother with Memorial Project Focused on Compassion and Activism
item.description: Tom Morello, guitarist of Rage Against The Machine, has launched the Mary Morello Memorial Project to honor his late mother, who passed away at the age of 102. The initiative encourages people to supp...

item.title: Union minister urges parliamentary debate on exam irregularities amid youth protests
item.description: Union Education Minister JP Nadda called for a parliamentary discussion on alleged examination paper leaks, urging the Opposition not to politicise the issue. He highlighted cases in non-BJP governed ...

item.title: BC Wildfires Close Key Highways, Disrupt Travel Between Lower Mainland and Interior
item.description: Wildfires in British Columbia have forced the closure of two major highways, disrupting travel between the Lower Mainland and the Interior. The Anderson Creek wildfire, located south of Hicks, has gro...

item.title: French modelling scout linked to Epstein dies after alleged crimes and legal scrutiny
item.description: Daniel Siad, a French modelling scout with ties to the late convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, has been found dead at his home outside Paris, prosecutors confirmed. Siad's body was discovered on ...

item.title: GGML Mine Success Highlights Tanzanian Leadership in Mining Sector
item.description: The article discusses Tanzania's significant achievement at the GGML mine, emphasizing the country's growing influence in the mining sector. With 80% of the mine's leadership being Tanzanians, this ma...

item.title: Samsung Unveils New Galaxy Z Series Smartphones with Advanced Features and Competitive Pricing
item.description: Samsung has launched its latest Galaxy Z Series smartphones, featuring foldable designs, enhanced AI capabilities, and improved camera systems. The series includes three models: the Galaxy Fold8 Ultra...

item.title: Champions League final chaos: PSG's victory sparks Paris riots and Arsenal's bitter defeat
item.description: The Champions League final between Paris Saint-Germain (PSG) and Arsenal ended in controversy, with celebrations of PSG's win turning into violent riots across Paris. Tear gas was deployed as crowds c...

item.title: Republican Blocks Bill to Halt Retrofit of Qatari-Gifted Air Force One
item.description: A Republican senator has blocked a Democratic proposal aimed at preventing the Pentagon from continuing modifications on a luxury jet gifted by Qatar. The aircraft, a Boeing 747-800 valued at approxim...

item.title: Yanga Set to Begin New Season Without Key Players
item.description: The news article discusses the Tanzanian football club Yanga's preparations for the new season, highlighting the absence of key players such as Mzize, Job, and Pacome. These players, who were previous...

item.title: House Prosecution Opposes Senate-Funded Catering for Sara Duterte's Legal Team
item.description: The House prosecution panel has rejected a request by Vice President Sara Duterte's office for the Senate to cover catering expenses for her defense team during the impeachment trial. House Deputy Spe...

item.title: Nigeria's Mental Health Crisis: Tied to Economic and Social Challenges
item.description: The article highlights the growing mental health crisis in Nigeria, linking it to deep-rooted economic struggles, social inequalities, and systemic neglect. It emphasizes that mental health is not jus...

item.title: Protesters clash with police, internet suspended near Sansad Marg during CJP demonstration
item.description: Amid ongoing protests led by the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP), tensions flared near Sansad Marg in Delhi on July 22, 2026. A crowd turned unruly near Park Hotel, prompting security forces to deploy tea...

item.title: Canada-U.S. Bridge Revenue Deal Details Spark Discrepancy Concerns
item.description: The article details a revenue-sharing agreement between Canada and the United States for the Gordie Howe Bridge, highlighting conflicting descriptions of the deal by officials. Canadian Prime Minister...

item.title: Security Guard Denies Accidental Shooting During 2022 Ambush Trial
item.description: A security guard involved in the 2022 ambush of taxi magnate Joe Sibanyoni has denied accidentally shooting him during the attack. Bethwell Cele, the guard, testified at the Johannesburg High Court du...

item.title: Clayface Movie Trailer: Standalone Batman Film Features Disfigured Shapeshifter Seeking Revenge
item.description: The article presents the trailer for James Watkins' upcoming standalone Batman film, 'Clayface,' which follows a disfigured shapeshifter seeking revenge. The film stars Tom Rhys Harries, Naomi Ackie, ...

item.title: William Shatner and Daughter Melanie Survive Stage 4 Cancer Thanks to Groundbreaking Treatments
item.description: William Shatner, 95, and his daughter Melanie, 61, have both survived stage 4 cancer after undergoing intensive treatments. Shatner, best known for his role as Captain Kirk in Star Trek, was diagnosed...

item.title: States Push for ICE Officer Identification Amid Legal Challenges
item.description: The article details the legal battle between states and the Trump administration over laws requiring ICE agents to identify themselves during immigration raids. Federal lawyers argue that anti-mask la...

item.title: Mumbai protesters allege police surveillance, repeated detentions and intimidation during youth demonstrations
item.description: The article reports allegations that the Mumbai Police have adopted extensive surveillance and preventive measures to discourage young people from participating in protests linked to the nationwide ag...
